summary_one_line: Valid ELF magic at bytes 0-3 plus byte 0x05 (EI_DATA) holding a value other than 1 (LSB) or 2 (MSB) to reach the default arm.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Bytes 0x00-0x03 must equal the literal magic 7f 45 4c 46 (\177ELF) so the memcmp guard at line 452 passes, AND byte 0x05 (ident[EI_DATA]) must hold a value other than ELFDATA2LSB(1) or ELFDATA2MSB(2) so the switch at line 468 falls through to the default arm at line 475.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winning cmplog seeds keep the magic 7f 45 4c 46 at offsets 0x00-0x03 and place a non-{1,2} value at offset 0x05 (e.g. seed 1 has 0x30, seed 2 has 0x00, seed 3 has 0xe7), so EI_DATA misses both case labels and hits default.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds also keep the magic but set offset 0x05 to either 0x01 or 0x02 in every sample, so EI_DATA always matches case ELFDATA2LSB or ELFDATA2MSB and the switch never reaches default.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLog buffer for the switch comparison on ident[EI_DATA] and substitutes the recorded compared operand at the input offset, but unlike a single magic-number gate the winning move is to make the byte NOT equal the case labels; I2S surfaces the magic prefix (7f 45 4c 46) keeping line 452 satisfied while the switch on a single byte is escaped via a non-matching value, a combination cmplog reaches and naive's blind havoc rarely lands while preserving the magic.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the default arm of the switch on ident[EI_DATA] at elf.cc:475.
Reaching the default requires passing the magic memcmp guard and missing both EI_DATA case labels.
The switch dispatches on ident[EI_DATA] with cases ELFDATA2LSB and ELFDATA2MSB before default.
Winning cmplog seeds preserve the ELF magic 7f 45 4c 46 at offsets 0x00-0x03.
A winning seed places a non-{1,2} value (0xe7) at offset 0x05 so EI_DATA misses both case labels.
Loser naive seeds set offset 0x05 to 0x01 or 0x02 (a valid EI_DATA case), so the switch matches a case and never reaches default.
The byte-diff shows W offset 0x05 concentrated on non-{1,2} values while L offset 0x05 is dominated by 01/02.
The divergent-branches table shows the blocker default at line 475 taken only in W (T=60) and never in L (T=0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 475  T=60 F=0  T=0 F=2220  default:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which scans the input for an operand and substitutes the other CMP operand at those offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 34 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 51f1d6d54d767ae6` and observed 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 1 and 2; also ran it on seed 0989f57862fb3e41 whose leaf is itself a ParentInfo-only dash row plus one ancestor dash row at depth 1, both confirming I2S contribution to the winning seeds under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose switch dispatches on a single byte gated behind a preceding magic memcmp showing naive resolving the default arm as often as cmplog (no cmplog>naive divergence), which would mean I2S substitution of the magic prefix is not what enables the winner.

weakest_evidence_point: Whether I2S's advantage here is solving the magic memcmp prefix (a roadblock) versus the switch byte being escaped by chance, since reaching default requires a NON-matching byte that I2S does not directly synthesize.
